SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.automation

Class AdjustableParameter

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.automation.AdjustableParameter
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class AdjustableParameter extends Object implements Serializable
Describes a single adjustable parameter (a degree of freedom) exposed by a process simulation.

An adjustable parameter is an input that an agent or optimizer may change to influence the process. It is sourced either from a writable SimulationVariable of type SimulationVariable.VariableType.INPUT, or from an Adjuster unit operation. For adjuster-sourced parameters the getTargetUnitName() and getTargetProperty() fields make explicit what the parameter actually affects, eliminating the guesswork that arises when a handle name does not match the variable it drives.

Version:
1.0
Author:
NeqSim

  • Constructor Details

    • AdjustableParameter

      public AdjustableParameter(String name, String address, String unit, Double lowerBound, Double upperBound, String targetUnitName, String targetProperty, AdjustableParameter.Source source)
      Creates a new adjustable parameter descriptor.
      Parameters:
      name - the short, human-readable parameter name
      address - the stable dot-notation address used with ProcessAutomation.setVariableValue(String, double, String)
      unit - the unit of measure for the parameter, e.g. "bara", "C", "kg/hr"
      lowerBound - the lower bound of the parameter, or null if unbounded
      upperBound - the upper bound of the parameter, or null if unbounded
      targetUnitName - the name of the unit operation that the parameter actually affects, or null if the parameter affects its own owning unit
      targetProperty - the property the parameter actually drives toward, or null if not applicable
      source - where the parameter originates (AdjustableParameter.Source.INPUT_VARIABLE or AdjustableParameter.Source.ADJUSTER)

  • Method Details

    • getName

      public String getName()
      Returns the short, human-readable parameter name.
      Returns:
      the parameter name

    • getAddress

      public String getAddress()
      Returns the stable dot-notation address for this parameter.
      Returns:
      the parameter address

    • getUnit

      public String getUnit()
      Returns the unit of measure for this parameter.
      Returns:
      the unit string (may be empty if dimensionless)

    • getLowerBound

      public Double getLowerBound()
      Returns the lower bound of this parameter.
      Returns:
      the lower bound, or null if unbounded

    • getUpperBound

      public Double getUpperBound()
      Returns the upper bound of this parameter.
      Returns:
      the upper bound, or null if unbounded

    • getTargetUnitName

      public String getTargetUnitName()
      Returns the name of the unit operation this parameter actually affects.

      For an AdjustableParameter.Source.ADJUSTER-sourced parameter this is the adjusted equipment's name, which may differ from the parameter name. For an AdjustableParameter.Source.INPUT_VARIABLE parameter this is the owning unit's name.

      Returns:
      the affected unit name, or null if not applicable

    • getTargetProperty

      public String getTargetProperty()
      Returns the property this parameter actually drives.
      Returns:
      the affected property name, or null if not applicable

    • getSource

      public AdjustableParameter.Source getSource()
      Returns where this parameter originates.
      Returns:
      the parameter source

    • toJsonObject

      public com.google.gson.JsonObject toJsonObject()
      Serializes this parameter to a JsonObject.
      Returns:
      a JSON object describing this adjustable parameter

    • toJson

      public String toJson()
      Serializes this parameter to a JSON string.
      Returns:
      a JSON string describing this adjustable parameter

    • toString

      public String toString()
